name: TAPS 24/7 Helpline
whatItProvides: Round-the-clock crisis intervention, emotional support, resources, and connection to care. Available at 800-959-TAPS (8277).

name: Join the TAPS Family
whatItProvides: Direct intake process to register for TAPS support, grief resources, peer matching, seminars, and counseling support.

name: Connect a Survivor to TAPS
whatItProvides: Referral tool for military leadership, casualty officers, healthcare providers, or friends to request outreach for a grieving survivor.

name: TAPS Online Survivor Portal
whatItProvides: Online hub where registered survivors can manage events, sign up for programs, message mentors, and read grief resources.

name: TAPS Survivor Support Hub
whatItProvides: The primary landing page for anyone grieving the death of a military or veteran loved one, regardless of relationship, circumstances, or cause of death.

name: One-to-One Peer Support
whatItProvides: Pairs newly bereaved survivors with trained peer mentors who are further along in their grief journey for support and connection.

name: Benefit & Financial Assistance
whatItProvides: Expert assistance in navigating federal survivor benefits, insurance claims, social security, burial allowances, and emergency relief.

name: Education Assistance Services
whatItProvides: Dedicated team helping survivors search for scholarships, utilize Fry Scholarship / Chapter 35 DEA benefits, and request university advocacy.

name: Connection to Grief Counseling
whatItProvides: Connects survivors to professional grief therapists, Vet Centers, community mental health clinics, and support groups.

name: Suicide Loss Postvention
whatItProvides: Specialized postvention program offering suicide-loss-specific peer groups, literature, suicide grief training, and national retreats.

name: Grief and Loss Education Library
whatItProvides: Articles, videos, webinars, and educational publications addressing coping mechanisms, traumatic grief, and child/teen bereavement.

name: In Your Community Local Support
whatItProvides: Helps survivors locate regional care groups, local gatherings, and community-based grief organizations close to home.

name: TAPS Casework Support
whatItProvides: BURIAL, records, paperwork, healthcare coordination (TRICARE), credit counseling, and civil legal assistance referrals for survivors.

name: Caregiver to Survivor Support
whatItProvides: Transition support for military caregivers grieving the loss of the service member or veteran they spent years caring for.

name: TAPS Toxic Exposure / PACT Act Resources
whatItProvides: Guidance on toxic exposure survivors benefits under the PACT Act, including VA dependency compensation claims for exposure-related deaths.
